enhancements.title: Chat menu tucked into a quick-access drawer
enhancements.detail: Your chat conversation now fills the whole screen. The First Steps, Front Desk, Look Up, and Briefings options you used to see on the left are now inside a slide-out drawer you open with a ☰ button at the bottom-left of your message box.
enhancements.answer: Rep Assist now opens straight into your full-width conversation instead of showing a side menu by default. To get to First Steps, Front Desk, Look Up, or Briefings, tap the ☰ button at the bottom-left of the composer to slide out the menu, pick what you need, and it automatically closes so you're back in the conversation. This works the same way on phone and desktop.

walkthrough.intro: Learn where the First Steps, Front Desk, Look Up, and Briefings options moved to, and how to get to them quickly.

steps.title: Open a chat
steps.detail: Rep Assist now opens directly into the full conversation view — you won't see a menu on the left side anymore.

steps.title: Tap the ☰ button
steps.detail: Look at the bottom-left of your message box and tap the ☰ icon to slide out the menu drawer.

steps.title: Browse the menu groups
steps.detail: You'll see the same four groups as before: First Steps, Front Desk, Look Up, and Briefings.

steps.title: Pick an option
steps.detail: Tap any item — the drawer closes automatically and drops you back into the chat with that action running.
steps.tip: If you tap outside the drawer or hit close, it dismisses without doing anything.

enhancements.title: See the whole store queue at a glance from the top bar
enhancements.detail: A new badge in the top bar always shows how many customers are waiting, how many are currently being helped, and how many are picking up online orders — it updates on its own every few seconds. Tap it to open a full queue view with waiting customers, in-progress visits, pickup orders, and today's scheduled appointments.
enhancements.answer: Look at the top bar for a small badge showing counts like waiting, active, and ISPU (in-store pickup) — it refreshes automatically so you don't have to keep checking manually. Tap the badge to open a drawer with four groups: customers waiting, customers currently being assisted, pickup orders that are ready or need to be pulled, and today's remaining scheduled appointments. The 'View queue' card in chat now shows those same upcoming appointments too, listed as 'Upcoming' rows.

walkthrough.intro: Learn how to read the new Live Queue badge and open the full queue drawer to see everyone waiting, being helped, or scheduled today.

steps.title: Glance at the top bar badge
steps.detail: You'll see a compact count like '3 wait / 2 active / 1 ISPU' — this updates automatically roughly every 20 seconds, no refresh needed.

steps.title: Tap the badge
steps.detail: This opens the Live Queue drawer with the full breakdown.

steps.title: Check the Waiting and Being Assisted sections
steps.detail: See exactly who's waiting for help and who a teammate (or you) is already helping.

steps.title: Check the Pickup section
steps.detail: This shows online orders that still need to be pulled and ones that are staged and ready for pickup.

steps.title: Scroll to Upcoming appointments
steps.detail: Today's remaining scheduled appointments show at the bottom, so you know who else is coming in.
steps.tip: You can also type 'view queue' in chat to get this same appointment info as a card.

enhancements.title: Live Listen now scores your visits and coaches you afterward
enhancements.detail: After you end a Live Listen session, Rep Assist grades the conversation against the store's official playbook and gives you a 1-5 star score with what you did well and what to work on. A new Coaching tile lets you pull up past graded visits and get a suggested script for doing better next time.
enhancements.answer: When you stop a Live Listen session, Rep Assist compares the conversation to the current playbook guidelines and shows a Playbook Score card with a star rating, a quick headline, and a breakdown of strengths and gaps. Tap the new Coaching tile in the sidebar to revisit any recent graded visit and get a personalized tip plus a suggested script — this is especially helpful if you missed positioning something the customer was eligible for.

walkthrough.intro: Here's how to see your Playbook Score after a visit and pull up coaching to improve next time.

steps.title: Start a Live Listen session
steps.detail: Tap Listen in the chat composer and pick the checked-in customer you're helping.

steps.title: Have your normal conversation
steps.detail: Talk with the customer as usual — Live Listen quietly follows along in the background.

steps.title: End the session
steps.detail: Tap Stop when you're done talking with the customer.

steps.title: Check your Playbook Score card
steps.detail: A card appears right in the thread showing your star rating, a quick headline, and what you did well versus what to work on.
steps.tip: Read the gaps section closely — it often points out an opportunity you didn't mention.

steps.title: Open the Coaching tile
steps.detail: Tap the 🎯 Coaching tile in the menu drawer to see a list of your recent graded visits.

steps.title: Pick a visit for a suggested script
steps.detail: Select any past visit to get a specific tip and a suggested script for how to handle it better next time.

enhancements.title: Spot sales opportunities before you even start talking
enhancements.detail: Rep Assist now flags customers who may be eligible for things like an upgrade promo, Fiber Home Internet, or Fixed Wireless Internet with a 💡 badge in the check-in queue, and Live Listen calls out those opportunities right when you start the session.
enhancements.answer: Look for the 💡 badge on a customer's entry in the front-desk queue card — it means Rep Assist has spotted something they may qualify for, like an upgrade promo or a home internet offer. When you start Live Listen with that customer, it will remind you out loud what to look for, and if you don't end up mentioning it, your visit score and coaching feedback will call that out afterward.

walkthrough.intro: Learn to spot the opportunity badge and make sure you position it during the visit.

steps.title: Open the queue card
steps.detail: Pull up the front-desk queue to see who's checked in and waiting.

steps.title: Look for the 💡 badge
steps.detail: A lightbulb badge next to a customer's name means Rep Assist thinks they may be eligible for an upgrade promo, Fiber Home Internet, or Fixed Wireless Internet.

steps.title: Claim the customer
steps.detail: Tap Assist on their entry, or start a Live Listen session with them.

steps.title: Listen for the callout
steps.detail: When Live Listen starts, it will remind you which opportunity to bring up during the conversation.
steps.tip: Make a point of mentioning it early — it's easy to forget once the conversation moves on.

steps.title: Check your coaching feedback afterward
steps.detail: If you didn't position the opportunity, your Playbook Score and coaching tip will flag it as a gap to work on next time.

enhancements.title: Live Listen gets a docked transcript, smarter lookups, and visit-summary emails
enhancements.detail: The live transcript now stays pinned right above your message box instead of scrolling away, so it's easier to follow. Live Listen also uses the customer's known account or order info automatically (skipping extra questions), and when you end a session you can send the customer a short AI-written visit summary by email with one tap.
enhancements.answer: When you check in a customer with an account or order number, Live Listen now carries that straight into the conversation so it can look things up without stopping to ask you for it again. The transcript window is now docked above the chat box so it doesn't scroll out of view on your phone. When you stop a session, Rep Assist writes up a quick, customer-friendly recap of the visit and shows it as a card — tap 'Send visit summary' and it emails that recap to the customer (this uses a new email preference in Settings).

walkthrough.intro: See how the improved Live Listen experience keeps the transcript visible and can email the customer a recap.

steps.title: Check in the customer with their info
steps.detail: When checking them in, add their account or order number if you have it.

steps.title: Start Live Listen
steps.detail: Tap Listen and select the customer — Rep Assist automatically carries their account/order info into the session so it won't ask again.

steps.title: Watch the docked transcript
steps.detail: Notice the transcript now stays pinned right above your message box the whole time instead of scrolling away.

steps.title: Stop the session
steps.detail: Tap Stop once the conversation wraps up.

steps.title: Review the visit summary card
steps.detail: Rep Assist shows a short, customer-friendly recap of what happened during the visit.

steps.title: Send it to the customer
steps.detail: Tap 'Send visit summary' to email the recap directly to the customer in one tap.
steps.tip: Make sure the customer's email subscription preference is turned on in Settings.

enhancements.title: Check in walk-in customers and manage the front-desk queue
enhancements.detail: A new Check In option lets you log a walk-in customer's name, phone, and reason for the visit, plus their account or order number if you have it. Everyone waiting shows up in a queue card, and tapping Assist on an entry pulls their info straight into chat so you can start helping them right away.
enhancements.answer: Use the new Check In button to log a walk-in customer with their visit reason, contact info, and account or order number if known, and they'll appear in the store's queue card in the order they arrived. When you're ready to help someone, tap Assist on their entry to claim it — Rep Assist drops you into the normal chat flow with their name and details already filled in, so you don't have to ask twice or retype anything.

walkthrough.intro: Learn how to check in a walk-in customer and pick them up from the queue.

steps.title: Open the menu drawer
steps.detail: Tap the ☰ button at the bottom-left of the composer, then choose Front Desk to see the Check In option.

steps.title: Tap Check In
steps.detail: This opens the intake form for logging a walk-in customer.

steps.title: Fill in the customer's details
steps.detail: Enter their name, phone number, and reason for the visit, plus their account or order number if you have it.

steps.title: Submit the check-in
steps.detail: The customer now appears in the store's queue card in the order they arrived.

steps.title: Find them in the queue
steps.detail: Open the queue card, or the Live Queue badge in the top bar, to see everyone currently waiting and their position in line.

steps.title: Tap Assist to claim them
steps.detail: Tap Assist on their entry — Rep Assist drops you straight into chat with their name and details already filled in.
steps.tip: No need to re-ask for info they already gave at check-in.

enhancements.title: AI-assisted Resolution Desk for ticket triage
enhancements.detail: Rep Assist can now scan your open and in-review tickets and sort them into three buckets: ones you can solve by sharing a help article, ones it can fix for you automatically, and ones that need to be flagged as a system problem. Each bucket comes with a one-tap action so you can clear tickets faster.
enhancements.answer: Tap Analyze on the Resolution Desk and Rep Assist looks at your open and in-review tickets, then groups them into three buckets: ones that just need a help article shared with the customer, ones Rep Assist can fix automatically (like a stuck activation or a blocked order), and ones that look like a real system problem that needs an engineering ticket filed. Each bucket has a one-click action button, so you can work through a lot of tickets quickly instead of handling every one from scratch.

walkthrough.intro: Practice using the Resolution Desk to quickly triage and clear your ticket backlog.

steps.title: Open the Resolution Desk
steps.detail: Tap the ☰ menu button and navigate to the Resolution Desk section.

steps.title: Tap Analyze
steps.detail: Rep Assist scans your open and in-review tickets and sorts them into three buckets.

steps.title: Review the Education bucket
steps.detail: These tickets just need a help article shared with the customer — tap the one-click action to send it.

steps.title: Review the Agent Action bucket
steps.detail: These are tickets Rep Assist can fix automatically, like a stuck activation — tap the action to let it run the fix.
steps.tip: You'll still see what it did so you can explain it to the customer.

steps.title: Review the System Defect bucket
steps.detail: These look like a real system problem — tap the action to flag or file an engineering ticket for it.

enhancements.title: System health status indicator now catches problems automatically
enhancements.detail: The colored badge in the top bar still shows whether everything is running normally (green), there's a known issue (yellow), or a serious outage (red), and tapping it shows what's affected and live connection speed to each server region. Now it can also flip itself to yellow and pop up a toast on your screen automatically if Rep Assist's automated fixes start failing more than usual — even before an admin notices.
enhancements.answer: The health badge at the top of the screen turns yellow or red when there's a known issue, and tapping it opens a panel with details, any workaround, and live round-trip times to the East, Central, and West server regions. On top of admins posting status changes, the system now watches itself in the background — if automated troubleshooting starts failing more often than normal, it automatically marks the system degraded and pushes a toast notification to your screen without waiting for a human to spot it. An admin still has to clear that warning manually once the issue is resolved.

walkthrough.intro: Learn to read the system health badge and understand what an automatic alert means.

steps.title: Glance at the top bar badge
steps.detail: Green means everything's normal, yellow means a known issue, red means a serious outage.

steps.title: Tap the badge for details
steps.detail: A panel opens showing what's affected, any workaround, and live connection speed to the East, Central, and West server regions.

steps.title: Watch for an automatic toast alert
steps.detail: If Rep Assist's own automated fixes start failing more than usual, it can flip itself yellow and pop up a toast notification without an admin posting anything.
steps.tip: This means the system caught the problem itself — no need to report it, just keep working carefully until it clears.

steps.title: Wait for it to clear
steps.detail: An admin will manually clear the warning once the underlying issue is resolved.

suggestions: Where did the Front Desk and Look Up menu go?
How do I check in a walk-in customer now?
What does the 💡 badge in the queue mean?
